summary: 06:00Z cycle. SPRINT COMPLETE. All 4 false-positive fix issues resolved and merged: #1209 (PR #1243), #1202 (PR #1252), #1208 (PR #1238), #1139 (PR #1274). Tests: 4442/4442 passing. 0 open PRs. Previous escalations cleared. Transitioning to next sprint: KE-2 Canonical Action Normalization (#917). Swarm P0 alert #1289 (credit exhaustion) is an ops issue — monitoring, not a kernel engineering blocker.

mergedThisCycle.title: fix(matchers): safe-subshell allowlist — prevent $(date) false positives (closes #1139)
mergedThisCycle.note: stripSafeSubshells() strips 9 read-only subshell families before destructive scan. 20 tests. ReDoS-safe [^)(]* patterns. Superseded #1255.

prBudget.note: 0 open PRs — squad queue is clean.

triage.p0Note: #1289 Swarm Health Alert — 26.5% agent failure rate from credit/quota exhaustion across all 4 driver types. kernel-sr had 7 failures (budget_exhausted). Root cause: ops-level API credits depleted. Not a kernel engineering issue. Monitoring.

nextSprintIssues.title: ROADMAP: KE-2 Canonical Action Normalization (ActionContext)
nextSprintIssues.note: Unstarted 7 days. Blocks v3.0 and Phase 7. Sub-tasks: design ActionContext contract, Claude tool-call adapter, normalize policy engine to consume only ActionContext, benchmark 50-100µs.

nextSprintIssues.title: [dogfood] PreToolUse hook says 'allowed by default' but exits non-zero, blocking GitHub writes after first call
nextSprintIssues.note: Hook emits 'allowed by default | (attempt 1/3)' with non-zero exit — Claude Code treats non-zero PreToolUse as block. First gh issue create succeeds; subsequent writes blocked. Likely a state management bug in pre-hook return code logic.

escalations.description: Swarm health P0: 26.5% agent failures (35/132) due to credit/quota exhaustion. kernel-sr: 7 failures. pr-merger-agent-cloud: 30 failures in 24h with circuit breaker not preventing rapid retries on credit errors. Root cause is depleted API budgets for all 4 driver types.
recommendedActions: Replenish API credits for claude-code, copilot, gemini, codex
Fix pr-merger-agent-cloud circuit breaker: detect credit_error type and pause agent 30+ min before retry
Investigate 34 agents on other hosts missing from health check

metrics.sprintSummary: Sprint fully complete. 4/4 issues closed: #1209 (PR #1243), #1202 (PR #1252), #1208 (PR #1238), #1139 (PR #1274). All merged. Total sprint: ~56 new tests added across matchers, kernel, invariants.

testHealth.note: All passing. Gap from #1274 (20 tests in command-scanner) closes on merge — already merged.

sprintStatus.goal: ACHIEVED — Reduce false-positive governance denials blocking swarm agents

closedIssues.1202: CLOSED — PR #1252 (stripCommandWrappers: rtk/time/timeout prefix strip in aab.ts, 13 tests)

nextSprintPlan.goal: KE-2 Canonical Action Normalization — design ActionContext contract, build Claude tool-call adapter, normalize policy engine, benchmark 50-100µs. Parallel: fix #1276 hook false-block bug.

subTasks: Build Claude tool-call → ActionContext adapter in packages/adapters/src/claude-code.ts

copilot-cli.title: Fix hook exits non-zero with 'allowed by default' message
copilot-cli.note: Investigate claude-hook-wrapper.sh and apps/cli/src/commands/claude-hook.ts for state management bug causing non-zero exit on allowed-by-default decisions after first write operation in session.

escalationRules.notes: All prior escalations cleared. New P0 #1289 (credit exhaustion) is ops-owned — escalated to ops/director.

dogfoodObservations.description: All 4 false-positive fix issues resolved within 1 sprint cycle. Governance denial rate should drop significantly: heredoc (2,071 sessions), rtk prefix (3,825+ blocks), date subshell, MCP prefix all addressed. Clean test suite: 4442 passing.

Hook exits non-zero on 'allowed by default' decisions after first write in session. Impact: QA agents capped at 1 GitHub write per session. This is a regression risk as agents file issues and review PRs. Next sprint's copilot-cli assignment.
